differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (Cold agglutinin disease vs. Mixed)
Myelodysplastic syndrome with paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria clones
next_steps: Flow cytometry for CD55 and CD59 expression on neutrophils and erythrocytes to rule out PNH
Serum haptoglobin, free hemoglobin, and reticulocyte count
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Cold agglutinin titer and thermal amplitude testing
Serum ferritin, vitamin B12, and folate levels
Consultation with Hematology/Oncology for urgent assessment
Avoid unnecessary empiric steroids or antibiotics until a definitive diagnosis is established

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia with underlying marrow pathology (Evans syndrome/Mixed type)
Copper deficiency with secondary hematologic manifestations
next_steps: Flow cytometry (CD55/CD59) on peripheral blood to evaluate for PNH
Bone marrow biopsy and aspiration with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Serum haptoglobin, reticulocyte count, and peripheral blood smear review by a hematopathologist
Serum ferritin, triglyceride, and fibrinogen levels to assess for HLH criteria
Serum copper, ceruloplasmin, vitamin B12, and folate levels
Comprehensive rheumatologic workup including ANA and dsDNA
Consultation with Hematology-Oncology for marrow biopsy and definitive diagnostic guidance
Hold empiric corticosteroids until bone marrow and diagnostic workup are completed
